name: Microsoft Sentinel API
what_it_does: Cloud-native SIEM and SOAR platform built on Azure Log Analytics, offering incident management, threat hunting via KQL, and deep automation integration via Logic Apps and REST API.
use_cases: Build SOC automation agents that triage and enrich Sentinel incidents via REST API
Run KQL threat hunting queries programmatically to surface anomalies
Automate playbook execution and Logic App triggers from agent workflows
Manage analytic rules, watchlists, and threat indicators via API
Ingest custom security telemetry into Log Analytics workspaces for correlation
not_for: Organizations not on Azure — Sentinel is tightly coupled to the Azure ecosystem
Cost-sensitive workloads at high data volumes — per-GB ingestion costs scale quickly
best_when: Best when operating in an Azure-centric environment and building agents that need to drive incident response, enrichment, or automated remediation at scale.
avoid_when: Avoid when your infrastructure is multi-cloud or on-prem without Azure, or when data ingestion costs need to be minimized.

auth.notes: Azure AD OAuth 2.0 required; service principals with client credentials flow are standard for agent use. RBAC scopes map to Sentinel-specific roles (Reader, Responder, Contributor).

pricing.free_tier_limits: 90-day free trial with initial Azure credits; Log Analytics has 5GB/month free ingestion per workspace
pricing.paid_tiers: ~$2.46/GB ingested beyond free tier; Commitment Tiers available for discounts at 100GB+/day
pricing.notes: Costs are primarily driven by data ingestion volume. Auxiliary and Basic log tiers available at lower per-GB rates for high-volume, lower-priority data.

performance.rate_limits: Azure Resource Manager API: 12,000 reads and 1,200 writes per hour per subscription; Log Analytics query API has separate throttling

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Azure ARM errors are structured and well-documented with error codes; Log Analytics query errors include line/column hints for KQL issues.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET operations are fully idempotent. Incident updates use ETags for optimistic concurrency; agents must handle 412 Precondition Failed on conflicts.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth token refresh must be handled by agents — tokens expire after 1 hour
KQL queries via Log Analytics API are async for large result sets — poll for completion
ARM API throttling is per-subscription, not per-app — shared limits with other Azure services
Incident ETags change on every update; stale ETag causes 412 errors requiring re-fetch before retry

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure AD with RBAC provides fine-grained scope control. FedRAMP High authorized. Managed identity support eliminates static credential handling for agents running in Azure.
